body_txt: Loss is a difficult thing to deal with, regardless of whether it's already happened or still in progress. Faced with the death of friends, family, significant others, etc., it's not unknown for some characters to struggle with accepting the inevitable. Some may fall into denial, refusing to acknowledge their loss, sometimes to the extent of denying that their loved one is even dead or dying. Others may resort to increasingly desperate coping strategies to avoid feeling the full extent of the grief, sometimes to the point of addiction. Others are hit hard by depression and lose all motivation in life. Whatever the case, the root cause is the same: the inability to Let Go. In fiction, this can form a character arc in which an individual afflicted by this crippling sense of loss gradually manages to let go of those they have lost and move on with their lives. A not uncommon element of characters with a Lost Lenore ; may tie in with Death Is a Sad Thing in the case of younger characters. May involve Five Stages of Grief , but not always. Compare Her Heart Will Go On . Contrast Excessive Mourning .
